title: Festuca riloensis
taxonomy: Festuca riloensis is classified within the family Poaceae. This classification further categorizes it as a species of grass within the genus Festuca. The genus Festuca is part of the subfamily Pooideae and is commonly known as fescue grass. This classification places Festuca riloensis within the order Poales. It is native to the Balkan Peninsula, specifically in Rila Mountain.

intro: Festuca riloensis, also known as Rilo fescue, is a species of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, offering several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help in reducing stress and improving overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Festuca riloensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and respiratory problems.
